text: Using HTTPS across your website ensures all data transferred between users and servers is encrypted. For authentication, strong password policies and multi-factor login protect user accounts. Input validation is critical—never trust user data without checking it. Validate and sanitize all input to block attacks like SQL injection and cross-site scripting (XSS). Common vulnerabilities, including these injection flaws and insecure session management, can expose sensitive information. By combining secure coding practices, regular updates, and HTTPS, you significantly reduce the risk of breaches. Remember, security is not optional in web development; it is a fundamental requirement for safe and reliable web applications.
